title: Saving Copilot chat session history (audit trail)
text: typical workflow looks like: Export Copilot Chat session .zip file. Run extract_copilot_chat() write (update) dev/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD[_topic].md file. Re-run export + extract_copilot_chat() throughout day; function create timestamped backups. Periodically run cleanup_session_backups() delete old backups.

title: Saving Copilot chat session history (audit trail)
code: library(reproducibleai)

what: A runnable example (using a synthetic export zip)
title: Saving Copilot chat session history (audit trail)
text: make vignette runnable without requiring actual Copilot export, generate tiny zip mimics expected structure: markdown file root zip archive (extract_copilot_chat() looks )
code: tmp <- tempdir()  # Create a minimal Copilot-like export zip in a temp folder export_dir <- file.path(tmp, "copilot_export_example") dir.create(export_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)  md_path <- file.path(export_dir, "copilot-chat.md") writeLines(c(   "# Example Copilot Chat Export",   "",   "This is a synthetic chat export used for the vignette.",   "",   "- Question: How do I do X?",   "- Answer: Here's how..." ), md_path)  zip_path <- file.path(tmp, "copilot_export_example.zip")  # zip() works most reliably with relative paths, so temporarily setwd() old_wd <- getwd() setwd(export_dir) utils::zip(zipfile = zip_path, files = basename(md_path)) setwd(old_wd)  file.exists(zip_path) #> [1] TRUE

title: Saving Copilot chat session history (audit trail)
text: real project, ’ll typically use sessions_dir = "dev/sessions" (default). vignette, write temp directory don’t modify project.
code: sessions_dir <- file.path(tmp, "dev", "sessions")  session_path <- extract_copilot_chat(   zip_path = zip_path,   session_date = "2026-04-14",   topic = "documentation",   sessions_dir = sessions_dir,   backup = TRUE,   quiet = TRUE )  session_path #> [1] "C:\\Users\\B5PMMMPD\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\1\\Rtmp6Fe9rq/dev/sessions/2026-04-14_documentation.md" readLines(session_path, warn = FALSE)[1:6] #> [1] "# Example Copilot Chat Export"                          #> [2] ""                                                       #> [3] "This is a synthetic chat export used for the vignette." #> [4] ""                                                       #> [5] "- Question: How do I do X?"                             #> [6] "- Answer: Here's how..."

what: Updating the same session (and creating backups)
title: Saving Copilot chat session history (audit trail)
text: export later re-run extract_copilot_chat() session_date + topic, existing file overwritten backup created dev/sessions/.backups/. demonstrate , update synthetic markdown inside zip extract . List session files backups:
code: # Update the synthetic markdown and re-zip writeLines(c(   "# Example Copilot Chat Export (updated)",   "",   "This simulates a later export from the same day.",   "",   "- New item added later in the session." ), md_path)  old_wd <- getwd() setwd(export_dir) utils::zip(zipfile = zip_path, files = basename(md_path)) setwd(old_wd)  # Extract again -> should create a backup of the previous session file session_path2 <- extract_copilot_chat(   zip_path = zip_path,   session_date = "2026-04-14",   topic = "documentation",   sessions_dir = sessions_dir,   backup = TRUE,   quiet = TRUE )  identical(session_path, session_path2) #> [1] TRUE list.files(sessions_dir, full.names = FALSE) #> [1] "2026-04-14_documentation.md"  backup_dir <- file.path(sessions_dir, ".backups") list.files(backup_dir, full.names = FALSE) #> [1] "2026-04-14_documentation_backup_20260426_173300.md"

title: Saving Copilot chat session history (audit trail)
text: time, repeated exports can create many backups. Use cleanup_session_backups() delete backups older days_to_keep. First, dry run (deletion): actually delete eligible backups real project:
code: cleanup_session_backups(   sessions_dir = sessions_dir,   days_to_keep = 7,   dry_run = TRUE ) cleanup_session_backups(   sessions_dir = "dev/sessions",   days_to_keep = 7,   dry_run = FALSE )

title: Saving Copilot chat session history (audit trail)
text: consistent audit trail across projects: Store session transcripts dev/sessions/ Use YYYY-MM-DD[_topic].md naming (extract_copilot_chat() produces) Commit dev/sessions/*.md version control appropriate org Decide backup retention policy (e.g., keep 7 30 days) Periodically run cleanup_session_backups() (schedule )

title: Saving Copilot chat session history (audit trail)
text: Common issues: “Zip file found”: confirm path correct accessible. “.md file found zip archive root”: extract_copilot_chat() expects markdown file root zip. Multiple root .md files: function use first one (warn).

title: Reproducible chat instructions
text: reproducible chat session needs two things: Stable guidance (rules assistant follow), local record exactly guidance used. use_instructions() supports (2) copying chosen instruction modules known folder.

title: Reproducible chat instructions
text: Use instructions_available() see modules installed package: want resolved file paths:
code: library(reproducibleai)  instructions_available() #> [1] "chat-manual" "goals"       "quarto-book" "r-package"   "shiny-golem" #> [6] "user-manual" instructions_available(include_path = TRUE) #>        module #> 1 chat-manual #> 2       goals #> 3 quarto-book #> 4   r-package #> 5 shiny-golem #> 6 user-manual #>                                                                                                                                                         path #> 1 C:/Users/B5PMMMPD/AppData/Local/R/cache/R/renv/library/reproducibleai-f2dbcda7/windows/R-4.5/x86_64-w64-mingw32/reproducibleai/instructions/chat-manual.md #> 2       C:/Users/B5PMMMPD/AppData/Local/R/cache/R/renv/library/reproducibleai-f2dbcda7/windows/R-4.5/x86_64-w64-mingw32/reproducibleai/instructions/goals.md #> 3 C:/Users/B5PMMMPD/AppData/Local/R/cache/R/renv/library/reproducibleai-f2dbcda7/windows/R-4.5/x86_64-w64-mingw32/reproducibleai/instructions/quarto-book.md #> 4   C:/Users/B5PMMMPD/AppData/Local/R/cache/R/renv/library/reproducibleai-f2dbcda7/windows/R-4.5/x86_64-w64-mingw32/reproducibleai/instructions/r-package.md #> 5 C:/Users/B5PMMMPD/AppData/Local/R/cache/R/renv/library/reproducibleai-f2dbcda7/windows/R-4.5/x86_64-w64-mingw32/reproducibleai/instructions/shiny-golem.md #> 6 C:/Users/B5PMMMPD/AppData/Local/R/cache/R/renv/library/reproducibleai-f2dbcda7/windows/R-4.5/x86_64-w64-mingw32/reproducibleai/instructions/user-manual.md

title: Reproducible chat instructions
text: instructions_recipes() provides opinionated, maintained “known good” compositions.
code: recipes <- instructions_recipes() names(recipes) #> [1] "base"               "r_package"          "shiny_golem"        #> [4] "quarto_book"        "quarto_user_manual"  recipes$base #> [1] "chat-manual" "goals" recipes$r_package #> [1] "chat-manual" "goals"       "r-package" recipes$quarto_user_manual #> [1] "chat-manual" "goals"       "quarto-book" "user-manual"

what: Write instructions into a project folder
title: Reproducible chat instructions
text: real project, ’ll usually write dev/instructions/: vignette, write temporary directory nothing working directory modified: can inspect written files confirm guidance used:
code: use_instructions(c("chat-manual", "goals", "r-package")) tmp <- tempdir() dest <- file.path(tmp, "dev", "instructions")  paths <- use_instructions(   spec = c("chat-manual", "goals", "quarto-book", "user-manual"),   dest_dir = dest,   quiet = TRUE )  paths #> [1] "C:\\Users\\B5PMMMPD\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\1\\RtmpEjdDRY/dev/instructions/chat-manual.md"       #> [2] "C:\\Users\\B5PMMMPD\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\1\\RtmpEjdDRY/dev/instructions/goals.md"             #> [3] "C:\\Users\\B5PMMMPD\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\1\\RtmpEjdDRY/dev/instructions/quarto-book.md"       #> [4] "C:\\Users\\B5PMMMPD\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\1\\RtmpEjdDRY/dev/instructions/user-manual.md"       #> [5] "C:\\Users\\B5PMMMPD\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\1\\RtmpEjdDRY/dev/instructions/CHAT_INSTRUCTIONS.md" list.files(dest) #> [1] "chat-manual.md"       "CHAT_INSTRUCTIONS.md" "goals.md"             #> [4] "quarto-book.md"       "user-manual.md" cat(readLines(file.path(dest, "chat-manual.md"), warn = FALSE)[1:10], sep = "\n") #> # chat-manual — Chat Interaction Protocol (Manual / Review-First) #>  #> ## Scope: target repository (required) #> - Operate only on the **explicitly specified** target repository for this chat session. #> - If no target repo is explicitly provided in the current chat session, **STOP and ask me to specify it** (owner/repo). #> - Do not guess the repo from usernames, prior chats, or general context. #>  #> ## Permissions model (required) #> ### Allowed actions (read-only) #> You may use read-only inspection as needed to improve accuracy, including:

title: Reproducible chat instructions
text: selected modules written dev/instructions/, chat session can start single entrypoint file: dev/instructions/CHAT_INSTRUCTIONS.md entrypoint file documents: - instruction modules apply repository, - order read/applied. “entrypoint” pattern makes easy start new chat session reproducibly: refer one file, file declares selected modules.

title: Reproducible chat instructions
text: typical “start day” workflow might look like: Quarto technical manuals: c("chat-manual", "goals", "quarto-book", "user-manual") Shiny golem apps: c("chat-manual", "goals", "r-package", "shiny-golem") use_instructions(spec) commit dev/instructions/*.md reviewers can see exactly guidance governed chat session. “Read dev/instructions/CHAT_INSTRUCTIONS.md follow selected instruction modules.”

title: Reproducible chat instructions
text: use_instructions() validates requested modules exist. typo module name, error include list available modules can correct quickly.

title: SBOM for R Package Repositories (renv + GitHub Actions)
text: vignette documents configure R package repository generate Software Bill Materials (SBOM) part continuous integration (CI) using: renv reproducible dependency locking (renv.lock) GitHub Actions CI Anchore SBOM Action (Syft-based) generate SBOM file (SPDX JSON default) reproducibleai standardize configuration (use_sbom()) write additional environment metadata (write_sbom_env()) workflow designed : repeatable (steps across repos) auditable (SBOM bundle includes renv.lock + environment manifest) scalable (multi-language projects can share SBOM generation approach later)

title: SBOM for R Package Repositories (renv + GitHub Actions)
text: every pull request merges main, workflow generate artifact bundle : artifacts/sbom/ Typically including: sbom.spdx.json (SBOM generated Anchore action) <Package>_env_sha-<shortsha>.txt (environment manifest generated reproducibleai) renv.lock (copied bundle document dependency lock) Artifacts uploaded target retention 120 days, subject GitHub org/repo policy.

title: SBOM for R Package Repositories (renv + GitHub Actions)
text: repository must GitHub Actions enabled.

title: SBOM for R Package Repositories (renv + GitHub Actions)
text: SBOM generation workflow requires renv.lock. repo yet use renv, initialize package root: Commit resulting files: renv.lock renv/activate.R commit renv/library/.
code: install.packages("renv") renv::init()

code: reproducibleai::use_sbom()

previous_headings: Step-by-step: enable SBOM in an R package repo
title: SBOM for R Package Repositories (renv + GitHub Actions)
text: Merge commits open pull request confirm: sbom-r workflow runs successfully workflow run uploaded artifact containing artifacts/sbom/ bundle includes sbom.spdx.json, renv.lock, env manifest file

what: renv::restore() fails compiling packages (common with sf stacks)
title: SBOM for R Package Repositories (renv + GitHub Actions)
text: package depends sf, terra, units, xml2, etc., CI may fail due missing system libraries/headers Ubuntu runners. default workflow written use_sbom() installs baseline set Ubuntu packages (GDAL/PROJ/GEOS/udunits, etc.), may need extend depending dependency graph. Look errors like: fatal error: ...: file directory configure: error: ... found add corresponding Ubuntu apt-get package(s) workflow.

title: SBOM for R Package Repositories (renv + GitHub Actions)
text: Anchore SBOM Action defaults SPDX JSON output (format: spdx-json). CycloneDX can adopted later /required.

title: SBOM for R Package Repositories (renv + GitHub Actions)
text: stronger supply-chain controls, pin third-party actions full commit SHA rather moving tag like @v0. validating workflow, replace: uses: anchore/sbom-action@v0 specific commit SHA.

title: SBOM for R Package Repositories (renv + GitHub Actions)
text: environment manifest includes: Package name + version (DESCRIPTION) git SHA (GITHUB_SHA CI available) timestamp (UTC) R version + platform details sha256 hash renv.lock provides lightweight audit trail linking SBOM file exact locked dependency specification used repository.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Michael Dougherty. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Dougherty M (2026). reproducibleai: Reproducible AI. R package version 2026.04.17.
code: @Manual{,   title = {reproducibleai: Reproducible AI},   author = {Michael Dougherty},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 2026.04.17}, }

title: Reproducible AI
text: {reproducibleai} R package tools help teams adopt reproducible workflow AI-assisted projects. package contains set tools assist data science professionals adopt efficient workflows early stages AI adoption. hold opinionated view ethical use AI requires data scientists make well-informed, conscious decisions throughout analysis process. tools work progress designed help team experiment developing new methodologies AI-assisted workflows. tools help define repeatable framework chat instructions sessions across teams. Tools provided create transparent audit trail evaluate use AI. Core ideas: make chat sessions reproducible recording exact instruction modules used, encourage reviewability (clear inputs/outputs rationale), support auditable workflow teams adopt AI tooling.

what: Extract GitHub Copilot Chat from Export Zip — extract_copilot_chat
title: Extract GitHub Copilot Chat from Export Zip — extract_copilot_chat
text: Extracts main chat markdown file GitHub Copilot export zip archive saves dev/sessions/ date-based naming. Supports incremental updates throughout day via backup timestamp tracking.

title: Extract GitHub Copilot Chat from Export Zip — extract_copilot_chat
code: extract_copilot_chat(   zip_path,   session_date = Sys.Date(),   topic = NULL,   sessions_dir = "dev/sessions",   backup = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Extract GitHub Copilot Chat from Export Zip — extract_copilot_chat
text: zip_path Character. Path exported Copilot chat .zip file session_date Character Date. Date session (default: today). Format: "YYYY-MM-DD" Date object topic Character. Optional topic suffix filename (default: NULL) sessions_dir Character. Path sessions directory (default: "dev/sessions") backup Logical. Create timestamped backup overwriting? (default: TRUE) quiet Logical. Suppress informational messages? (default: FALSE)

title: Extract GitHub Copilot Chat from Export Zip — extract_copilot_chat
text: Character. Path extracted session file (invisibly)

title: Extract GitHub Copilot Chat from Export Zip — extract_copilot_chat
text: function designed incremental updates throughout work session: First call: Creates session file Subsequent calls: Overwrites latest export, creating backup Backups stored dev/sessions/.backups/ timestamps Original filename preserved easy reference

title: Extract GitHub Copilot Chat from Export Zip — extract_copilot_chat
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Call multiple times throughout the day - automatically handles updates extract_copilot_chat("~/Downloads/copilot_export.zip") # ... work for 2 hours ... extract_copilot_chat("~/Downloads/copilot_export.zip")  # Creates backup # ... handle brushfire ... extract_copilot_chat("~/Downloads/copilot_export.zip")  # Another backup  # Disable backups (not recommended for active sessions) extract_copilot_chat("~/Downloads/copilot_export.zip", backup = FALSE)  # Specify topic for multi-topic days extract_copilot_chat(   "~/Downloads/copilot_export.zip",   topic = "data_validation" ) } # }

what: Write selected instruction modules into a working directory — use_instructions
title: Write selected instruction modules into a working directory — use_instructions
text: Copies instruction modules shipped package (inst/instructions/) local folder (default: dev/instructions) reference reproducible chat sessions. Also writes entrypoint file (CHAT_INSTRUCTIONS.md) describing selected recipe read order.

title: Write selected instruction modules into a working directory — use_instructions
code: use_instructions(   spec,   dest_dir = "dev/instructions",   overwrite = TRUE,   write_entrypoint = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Write selected instruction modules into a working directory — use_instructions
text: spec Character vector module tokens, e.g. c("chat-manual", "goals", "r-package"). dest_dir Character. Destination directory (default: "dev/instructions"). overwrite Logical. Overwrite existing module files? (default: TRUE). Note: entrypoint file CHAT_INSTRUCTIONS.md always overwritten. write_entrypoint Logical. Write CHAT_INSTRUCTIONS.md? (default: TRUE). quiet Logical. Suppress informational messages? (default: FALSE).

title: Write selected instruction modules into a working directory — use_instructions
text: Character vector written file paths (invisibly), including entrypoint file write_entrypoint = TRUE.

what: Configure an R package repository to generate SBOM artifacts in CI — use_sbom
title: Configure an R package repository to generate SBOM artifacts in CI — use_sbom
text: Writes GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/sbom-r.yml) generates SBOM using Anchore's SBOM Action (Syft-based), writes additional environment metadata (copy renv.lock) artifacts/sbom/.

title: Configure an R package repository to generate SBOM artifacts in CI — use_sbom
code: use_sbom(dest_dir = ".", overwrite = FALSE, quiet = FALSE)

title: Configure an R package repository to generate SBOM artifacts in CI — use_sbom
text: dest_dir Character. Path repository root. Defaults ".". overwrite Logical. Overwrite existing workflow file? Defaults FALSE. quiet Logical. Suppress informational messages? Defaults FALSE.

title: Configure an R package repository to generate SBOM artifacts in CI — use_sbom
text: function intended run initial repository setup interactive session (similar usethis pattern). function : Validates dest_dir R package root (DESCRIPTION). Creates .github/workflows/ missing. Writes .github/workflows/sbom-r.yml (fails exists overwrite = FALSE). what: Write SBOM environment bundle metadata for an R package repo — write_sbom_env
title: Write SBOM environment bundle metadata for an R package repo — write_sbom_env
text: Writes lightweight environment manifest copies specified renv.lock SBOM artifact bundle directory (default: artifacts/sbom).

title: Write SBOM environment bundle metadata for an R package repo — write_sbom_env
code: write_sbom_env(   lockfile = "renv.lock",   out_dir = file.path("artifacts", "sbom"),   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Write SBOM environment bundle metadata for an R package repo — write_sbom_env
text: lockfile Character. Path renv.lock (default "renv.lock"). out_dir Character. Output directory SBOM bundle. Default file.path("artifacts", "sbom"). overwrite Logical. Overwrite existing bundle files? Defaults TRUE. quiet Logical. Suppress informational messages? Defaults FALSE.

title: Write SBOM environment bundle metadata for an R package repo — write_sbom_env
text: function intended called CI renv::restore() SBOM generation steps (e.g., Anchore SBOM Action). generate SBOM ; creates additional evidence documents environment associated SBOM artifact. Files written: <Package>_env_sha-<shortsha>.txt: environment manifest (Package/Version, git SHA, R version/platform, timestamp, lockfile hash) renv.lock: copy lockfile used
